---
title: "DAWA"
about:
  template: marquee
  image-shape: rectangle
  image-width: 100%
editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

# Digital Antibiotic stewardship and Training for Wahudumu wa Afya

**DAWA is a cluster-randomized trial evaluating the effectiveness of a
digital clinical decision support system, training and mentorship to
reduce antibiotic prescribing at primary care health facilities in
Zanzibar, Tanzania.**

## Background

Inappropriate antibiotic prescribing is widespread in primary care
settings in low- and middle-income countries, contributing to bacterial
antimicrobial resistance (AMR), and excess morbidity and mortality.
Antibiotic prescription is estimated to occur in over half of primary
care consultations in low- and middle-income countries.

Digital clinical decision support systems (CDSS) integrated within
electronic medical record (EMR) systems have shown potential to improve
antibiotic stewardship, yet existing tools have important limitations:
most target only children under five years of age, and many are not
embedded in the health facilities' digital infrastructure.

The DAWA project tests a comprehensive antibiotic stewardship
intervention — combining digital clinical decision support integrated
within the Zanzibar Electronic Medical Record (ZanEMR) system with
targeted training and monthly peer education meetings — at primary care
dispensaries on Unguja island, Zanzibar.

## Study Overview

| | |
|---|---|
| **Full title** | A cluster-randomized trial to assess the effectiveness of a digital clinical decision support system, training and mentorship to reduce antibiotic prescribing at primary care health facilities in Tanzania |
| **Short title** | Digital Antibiotic stewardship and Training for Wahudumu wa Afya (DAWA) |
| **Setting** | Public dispensaries, Unguja island, Zanzibar, Tanzania |
| **Design** | Three-arm parallel-group cluster randomized controlled trial |
| **Sample size** | 5,850 patients (1,950 per arm) |
| **Estimated first enrolment** | 1 November 2026 |
| **Registry** | To be registered on ClinicalTrials.gov |
| **Sponsor** | Centre for Primary Care and Public Health (Unisanté), University of Lausanne, Switzerland |

## Primary Objective

To evaluate the effectiveness of two antibiotic stewardship
interventions — both incorporating clinical decision support integrated
within an electronic medical record system, and one with an additional
monthly peer education component — to reduce antibiotic prescribing for
acute infections at primary care health facilities in Zanzibar, Tanzania.

## Study Arms

- **Arm 1 (Multifaceted Intervention):** ZanEMR-AS (digital clinical
  decision support integrated within ZanEMR) with initial group
  training, supported mentorship visits, and monthly Antibiotic
  Stewardship Club meetings.

- **Arm 2 (Digital Support Alone):** ZanEMR-AS with initial group
  training and supported mentorship visits.

- **Arm 3 (Usual Care):** Standard ZanEMR (no antibiotic stewardship
  features).

::: {style="background-color: #e8f5e9; padding: 1rem; border-radius: 8px; margin-top: 2rem;"}
*The DAWA project is a collaboration between the Centre for Primary Care
and Public Health (Unisanté), University of Lausanne; the Ifakara Health
Institute (IHI), Tanzania; and the Zanzibar Ministry of Health. It is
funded by the Swiss National Science Foundation (SNSF).*
:::
